mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce discount rates on equity cash flows, directly boosting forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 6.0 percent in more than 60 percent of instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical to industrial investment, compressing equity risk premiums and lifting S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce positive 60d forward returns in more than 50 percent of observed windows across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation regime where CPI exceeds 2.5 percent annualized, real yields fall and equity valuations expand, generating positive 20-day forward returns as markets price in accommodative policy.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when the CPI threshold is breached over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces recession probabilities and improves corporate earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns with a directional accuracy above 55 percent over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for three consecutive months, sustained employment gains trigger Fed tightening fears, compressing equity valuations and driving negative returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 65 percent of instances following three consecutive months of strong payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales coupled with a flat or declining federal funds rate signal strong consumer demand unimpeded by tightening credit, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and stable or decreasing FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 60d forward returns in more than 50 percent of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 1.5 percent month-over-month, supply-side capacity expansion supports corporate margins and drives positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or statistically insignificant when the 1-month INDPRO growth threshold is breached over a 3-year walk-forward test.
